LAW AND MOTION 302, RE: DEFENDANTS DIGNITY HEALTH, AND COMMONSPIRIT HEALTH'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OFF CALENDAR. THE HENDRICKSON DECLARATION INDICATES THAT THE PARTIES DID NOT MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE WITH REGARD TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH THE CODE. DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY AUGUST 25, 2023. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

JOSHUA R. HENDRICKSON, Esq., of THIERMAN BUCK LLP, 775.284.1500, for Plaintiffs.

TYLER J. JOHNSON, Esq., of SHEPPARD, MULLIN, RICHTER & HAMPTON LLP, 213.620.1780, for Defendants.

RE: Defendants' demurrer to plaintiff's second amended complaint [SAC], or in the alternative, motion to stay on the grounds of another similar action pending.

Ruling is ARGUED. At conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants' "demurrer to plaintiff's second amended complaint [SAC], or in the alternative, motion to stay on the grounds of another similar action pending" is denied.
This is a putative class and Private Attorney General Act (PAGA) action. It involves employees in one department at a Dignity Health hospital in Stockton. (SAC 5:16-19.) Plaintiff pleads that she and her cohort were ordered to work remotely starting with the Covid-19 pandemic, but have not been fully reimbursed for their internet and cellphone expenses. (Id. at 3:9-5:10.)
Defendants argue that this action should be abated or stayed because it is duplicative of an earlier-filed PAGA action in Los Angeles. I disagree. The words remote, pandemic or internet (or their derivatives) appear nowhere in that other action (of which judicial notice is taken). The other action seeks to cover a broad array of Dignity Health employees statewide, listing a number of wage and hour statutes in cookie cutter fashion. Our case is different and much more focused; it would be improper to abate or stay it.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

***After hearing, an order is signed.

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

JOSHUA R. HENDRICKSON, Esq., of THIERMAN BUCK LLP, (775) 284-1500, for Plaintiff.

TYLER J. JOHNSON, Esq., of SHEPPARD, MULLIN, RICHTER & HAMPTON LLP, (213) 620-1780, for Defendants.

RE: Defendant DIGNITY HEALTH, COMMONSPIRIT HEALTH's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Ruling is argued. At conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar. The Johnson declaration fails to show that the parties met and conferred "in person or by telephone" in compliance with CCP 430.41(a). The parties are ordered to comply with the code. Movants' response to the second amended complaint is now due by December 13, 2023.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar.
